title: podman-remote doesn't support newest ssh key exchange algorthims
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

According to `journalctl -u sshd` on a host machine, `podman system connection add` offers only these key exchange algorithms:

```txt
diffie-hellman-group14-sha1
diffie-hellman-group-exchange-sha1
diffie-hellman-group1-sha1
```

And according to Fedora, SHA1 is considered LEGACY since FF33: <https://fedoraproject.org/wiki/Changes/StrongCryptoSettings2>

There two work arounds for this situation:

1. [NOT GOOD BUT FAST]: Make Fedora use LEGACY KexAlgorithms: `ln -svf /usr/share/crypto-policies/LEGACY/opensshserver.txt /etc/crypto-policies/back-ends/opensshserver.config`
2. [NOT FAST BUT GOOD]: Generate ssh key (make sure key type is either `ecdsa` or `ed25519`) locally first and then upload it to AWS and use it for a new Fedora VM.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a Fedora CoreOS instance on AWS.

2. Let AWS generate a SSH key for it.

3. Try to add this VM using the SSH key to Podman machine list.

**Describe the results you received:**

`podman system connection add ssh://...` without socket path it failed with:

```txt
...
Error: ssh: handshake failed: ssh: unable to authenticate, attempted methods [none publickey], no supported methods remain
failed to connect
github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman/system/connection.getUDS
 /private/tmp/podman-20220402-80792-14flg0w/podman-4.0.3/cmd/podman/system/connection/add.go:214
github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman/system/connection.add
 /private/tmp/podman-20220402-80792-14flg0w/podman-4.0.3/cmd/podman/system/connection/add.go:115
...
```

`podman --connection=<CONNECTION> info` on previously added connection with socket path it failed with:

```txt
...
Error: unable to connect to Podman. failed to create sshClient: Connection to bastion host (ssh://<USER>@<HOST>:22/run/user/1000/podman/podman.sock) failed.: ssh: handshake failed: ssh: unable to authenticate, attempted methods [none publickey], no supported methods remain
github.com/containers/podman/v4/pkg/bindings.sshClient
 /private/tmp/podman-20220402-80792-14flg0w/podman-4.0.3/pkg/bindings/connection.go:292
github.com/containers/podman/v4/pkg/bindings.NewConnectionWithIdentity
 /private/tmp/podman-20220402-80792-14flg0w/podman-4.0.3/pkg/bindings/connection.go:106
github.com/containers/podman/v4/pkg/domain/infra.newConnection
 /private/tmp/podman-20220402-80792-14flg0w/podman-4.0.3/pkg/domain/infra/runtime_tunnel.go:25
github.com/containers/podman/v4/pkg/domain/infra.NewImageEngine
 /private/tmp/podman-20220402-80792-14flg0w/podman-4.0.3/pkg/domain/infra/runtime_tunnel.go:51
github.com/containers/podman/v4/cmd/podman/registry.NewImageEngine
 /private/tmp/podman-20220402-80792-14flg0w/podman-4.0.3/cmd/podman/registry/registry.go:53
...
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

Destination for the Podman service is successfully added.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes/No

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

AWS
MacOS 12.3.1
podman version 4.0.3

comments.body: @jwhonce PTAL
@baude FYI
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@jwhonce This is again caused by using builtin ssh in golang versus execing ssh.  Is there any change of improving the ssh bindings?
@rhatdan We could enhance the current ssh code to either exec ssh or use the golang library. Exec'ing would be slower, but would allow the developer to decide how to use ssh.
Would it be possible to do both?  Would it be make sense or be too much work?
I can help with this, btw. Doesn't look that difficult (🤞🏻 ) and quite important at the same time.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@sanyer did you ever get a chance to work on this?
@sanyer no, not yet really :-(
going to try and tackle this one now.

@rhatdan @jwhonce looking at this, I think it would make sense to first `exec.LookPath(ssh)` and if that fails, default to golang's package? basically everywhere we use `ssh.` we should also try to exec.
I am fine with that.  But I do worry about how well the ssh on MAC and Windows work versus Linux.
@rhatdan that is true. I guess if ssh fails as well we can default to the go libraries?

wait, shouldn't this fail for all `system connection add`'s using an ed25519 type key @sanyer ? I am trying to reproduce in a more local environment and cannot seem to.

I have drafted up a quick implementation using ssh, and it works for `add`! 

EDIT: @sanyer I booted up an AWS instance as described and connected using the .pem file given to me, and this worked for both `system connection add` and `info` using the new connection.
@cdoern I'll try again too then.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Yes, I still remember about it, will check it again this week.
@sanyer I am working on a fix for this
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
This should be solved by using the `--ssh=native` flag closing as completed
